title: CIL Civil — Full-length Mock 04
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India–UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India–UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
questions.solution: Gujarat has the longest coastline among Indian states.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The headquarters of Coal India Limited (CIL) is located in which city?
questions.solution: Coal India Limited is headquartered in Kolkata, West Bengal.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the largest coal-producing state in India in recent years?
questions.solution: Chhattisgarh has been India's largest coal-producing state, ahead of Odisha and Jharkhand.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Coal India Limited operates under which Union Ministry?
questions.solution: CIL functions under the Ministry of Coal, Government of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which subsidiary of CIL is the single largest coal-producing company?
questions.solution: South Eastern Coalfields Limited (SECL) is among CIL's largest producing subsidiaries.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.solution: Canberra is the capital of Australia, not Sydney or Melbourne.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the capital of Canada?
questions.solution: Ottawa is the national capital of Canada.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Mount Kilimanjaro, the highest mountain in Africa, is located in which country?
questions.solution: Mount Kilimanjaro lies in north-eastern Tanzania.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the largest planet in the Solar System?
questions.solution: Jupiter is the largest planet by both mass and diameter.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.solution: Iron oxide on its surface gives Mars a reddish appearance.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The SI unit of electric current is the:
questions.solution: The ampere (A) is the SI base unit of electric current.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which gas is most abundant in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.solution: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere by volume.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The powerhouse of the cell is the:
questions.solution: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's ATP, earning the 'powerhouse' name.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the chemical symbol of Gold?
questions.solution: Gold's symbol is Au (from Latin 'aurum'); Ag is silver.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the chemical symbol of Sodium?
questions.solution: Sodium's symbol is Na (from Latin 'natrium'); Sn is tin.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat covers 216 km downstream in 12 h and the same 216 km upstream in 18 h. Its speed in still water is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed adds the stream; upstream subtracts it. Still-water speed is the average of the two.
**Formula.** $u=\tfrac12\left[(b+s)+(b-s)\right]$
**Given.** Downstream 216 km in 12 h; upstream 216 km in 18 h.
**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{216}{12}=18$ km/h, upstream $=\tfrac{216}{18}=12$ km/h.
**Answer.** Still-water speed $u=\tfrac{18+12}{2}=15$ km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 3:36 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 3, M = 36.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times3-5.5\times36\right|=108^\circ$; smaller $=\min(108,360-108)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=108^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A trader marks goods 40% above cost, then gives a 25% discount. His net profit percentage is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net selling factor is the markup factor times the discount factor; profit% is that factor reduced by 1.
**Formula.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{m}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{d}{100}\right)$
**Given.** Markup m = 40%, discount d = 25%.
**Working.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{40}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{25}{100}\right)=1.05$.
**Answer.** Profit $=(f-1)\times100=5\%$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The difference between compound and simple interest on Rs 8000 at 5% per annum for 2 years is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Over 2 years the gap between compound and simple interest is the first year's interest-on-interest.
**Formula.** $CI-SI=P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$
**Given.** P = Rs 8000, r = 5% p.a., t = 2 yr.
**Working.** $=8000\left(\tfrac{5}{100}\right)^2$.
**Answer.** $CI-SI=$ Rs 20.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 4000 for 12 months and B invests Rs 8000 for 4 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 6000, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 4000×12 mo; B: Rs 8000×4 mo; profit Rs 6000.
**Working.** Ratio $=48000:32000$; share$_A=6000\times\tfrac{48000}{80000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 3600.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 100 L of milk. 20 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 2 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 100 L, x = 20 L, n = 2.
**Working.** $=100\left(1-\tfrac{20}{100}\right)^{2}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 64$ L.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man goes at 30 km/h and returns over the same route at 90 km/h. His average speed is:
questions.solution: Average speed = 2uv/(u+v) = 2×30×90/(30+90) = 45 km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is the average of the first 16 natural numbers?
questions.solution: Average of first n naturals = (n+1)/2 = (16+1)/2 = 8.5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number divided by 8 gives quotient 14 and remainder 3. The number is:
questions.solution: Number = divisor × quotient + remainder = 8×14+3 = 115.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A car covers a distance at 50 km/h in 5 hours. The distance is:
questions.solution: Distance = speed × time = 50 × 5 = 250 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 56 is divided in the ratio 2 : 5, the larger part is:
questions.solution: Larger part = 56 × 5/7 = 40.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A and B invest in the ratio 4 : 6. Out of a total profit of Rs 15000, A's share is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A 240 m long train crosses a pole in 18 seconds. Its speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The square root of 4225 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The average age of 6 persons is 30 years. If a person aged 42 leaves, the average of the remaining 5 is:
questions.solution: Total = 180; remaining = 180 − 42 = 138; average = 27.6.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 8 men complete a work in 18 days, 5 men will complete it in:
questions.solution: Men-days constant: 8×18 = 5×D, D = 28.8 days.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The compound ratio of 3 : 7 and 6 : 7 is:
questions.solution: Compound ratio = (3×6) : (7×7) = 18 : 49.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The percentage increase when a value rises from 40 to 52 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The HCF of 36 and 48 is:
questions.solution: The highest common factor of 36 and 48 is 12.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An item bought for Rs 1200 is sold for Rs 1320. The profit percentage is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 20% of a number is 40, the number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The LCM of 8 and 12 is:
questions.solution: The lowest common multiple of 8 and 12 is 24.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An article sold at Rs 450 incurs a 25% loss. Its cost price is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sum doubles itself in 20 years under simple interest. The annual rate is:
questions.solution: To double, SI = P, so rate = 100/T = 100/20 = 5%.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A can do a work in 15 days and B in 24 days. Working together they finish it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'ICE'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of I, C, E to get 17.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'PEN' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: PEN → RGP.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 5 km towards north, then turns east and walks 12 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(5² + 12²) = √169 = 13 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: X is the husband of Y. Z is the mother of Y. How is Z related to X?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Mother-in-law.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 2, 5, 11, 23, 47, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 47 × 2 + 1 = 95.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 18 people, X is 9th from the left. X's position from the right is:
questions.solution: Position from right = 18 − 9 + 1 = 10.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: The angle between the hands of a clock at 4:00 is:
questions.solution: Each hour = 30°; at 4:00 the hands are 4 × 30 = 120° apart.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: The only daughter of her mother is the woman herself, so she is the man's mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A=1, B=2, ... then the sum of the letters of BED is:
questions.solution: Add the alphabet positions of B, E, D to get 11.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: All roses are flowers. All flowers fade. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution: Both premises are universal affirmatives, so the chained conclusion 'All roses fade' follows.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 5 km north, then 12 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = √(5² + 12²) = 13 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these fractions is the largest?
questions.solution: Comparing decimal values, 4/5 is the largest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 3, 5, 9, 11?
questions.solution: 3, 5, 11 are prime; 9 is composite.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: GH, JK, MN, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter; after MN comes PQ.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: K, L, M, N, ?
questions.solution: Consecutive alphabet letters; after N comes O.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Dog : Kennel :: Horse : ?
questions.solution: Just as Kennel relates to Dog, Stable relates to Horse.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 1, 4, 9, 16, ?
questions.solution: These are perfect squares; next = 5² = 25.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person facing east turns 90° to the right. Now they face:
questions.solution: Turning right from east leads to South.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 2, 4, 8, 16, ?
questions.solution: Each term is multiplied by 2, so next = 16 × 2 = 32.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 5, 7, 11, 17, ?
questions.solution: Differences grow by 2; adding the next difference gives 25.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 2 January is a Friday, what day is 9 January?
questions.solution: 9 − 2 = 7 days, exactly one week, so it is again Friday.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: Cow, Goat, Tiger, Sheep?
questions.solution: Tiger belongs to a different category from the rest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 27, 64, 100, 125?
questions.solution: The others are perfect cubes; 100 is not.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: Y, W, U, S, ?
questions.solution: Letters decrease by 2 each time, so the next letter is Q.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'once in a blue moon' means:
questions.solution: 'Once in a blue moon' means something happening very rarely.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to bury the hatchet' means:
questions.solution: 'Bury the hatchet' means to end a quarrel and make peace.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to let the cat out of the bag' means:
questions.solution: It means to accidentally reveal a secret.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The plural of 'Child' is:
questions.solution: 'Child' has the irregular plural 'children'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The plural of 'Mouse' is:
questions.solution: 'Mouse' has the irregular plural 'mice'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Neither he nor I ___ wrong.'
questions.solution: With 'neither...nor', the verb agrees with the nearer subject 'I', so 'am'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Each of the students ___ a book.'
questions.solution: 'Each' is singular, so it takes 'has'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is ___ honest man.'
questions.solution: 'Honest' starts with a vowel sound, so 'an' is used.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She plays ___ violin beautifully.'
questions.solution: Musical instruments take the definite article 'the'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Passive voice of 'John wrote the letter.' is:
options: The letter was written by John
The letter is written by John
questions.solution: Past simple active becomes 'was written' in the passive.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Passive voice of 'She is reading a book.' is:
options: A book is being read by her
A book has been read by her
A book was read by her
A book is read by her
questions.solution: Present continuous active becomes 'is being read' in the passive.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Spot the error: 'He / don't / like / coffee.'
questions.solution: Third-person singular needs 'doesn't', not 'don't'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Spot the error: 'One of the boys / are / playing / outside.'
questions.solution: 'One of the boys' is singular, so it requires 'is'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'I ___ my homework already.'
questions.solution: 'Already' signals present perfect, so 'have done' is correct.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'By next year, she ___ here for a decade.'
questions.solution: A completed action by a future point uses the future perfect 'will have worked'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the word that means the opposite of 'Artificial'.
questions.solution: 'Artificial' is opposite to 'natural'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correct meaning of the word 'Frugal'.
questions.solution: 'Frugal' means careful with money, i.e. economical.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Abundant'.
questions.solution: 'Abundant' means existing in large quantity, i.e. plentiful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Brief'.
questions.solution: 'Brief' means short or concise.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Happy'.
questions.solution: 'Happy' is synonymous with 'joyful'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Enormous'.
questions.solution: 'Enormous' means very large, i.e. huge.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Diligent'.
questions.solution: 'Diligent' describes someone hard-working and careful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Candid'.
questions.solution: 'Candid' means open and frank in speech.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Vivid'.
questions.solution: 'Vivid' means bright, clear and intense.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Ancient'.
questions.solution: 'Ancient' (very old) is opposite to 'modern'.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the slope-deflection method, the primary unknowns are the:
questions.solution: Slope-deflection treats the unknown joint displacements (rotations/translations) as primary unknowns.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentation tanks in water treatment primarily remove:
questions.solution: Sedimentation removes settleable suspended solids by gravity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 2.5 m long carries a 10 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W × L = 10 × 2.5 = 25 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Contour lines on a map join points of:
questions.solution: A contour line connects points of equal elevation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention time of a tank of volume 300 m³ treating a flow of 100 m³/h is:
questions.solution: Detention time = Volume / Discharge = 300 / 100 = 3 hours.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of links in a 30 m metric chain is:
questions.solution: A 30 m metric chain has 150 links of 0.2 m each.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Camber on a road surface is provided mainly for:
questions.solution: Camber lets rainwater drain off the carriageway to the sides.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam carrying a UDL w over span L, the support (fixed-end) moment is:
questions.solution: Fixed-end moment for a UDL is wL²/12 at each support.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coagulant most commonly used in water treatment is:
questions.solution: Alum (aluminium sulphate) is the usual coagulant for removing fine suspended matter.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section modulus of a rectangular section 250 mm wide and 200 mm deep is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Marshall stability test is used for:
questions.solution: The Marshall test determines optimum bitumen content in bituminous mixes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slump test on fresh concrete measures its:
questions.solution: The slump test is a measure of the workability (consistency) of fresh concrete.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superelevation for a design speed of 80 km/h and radius 400 m (e = V²/127R) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an open channel, the specific energy is minimum at the:
questions.solution: The specific energy curve attains its minimum value at the critical depth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the fore bearing of a line is 45°, its back bearing is:
questions.solution: Back bearing = fore bearing ± 180° = 225°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The broad gauge of Indian Railways measures:
questions.solution: Indian Broad Gauge is 1676 mm; 1435 mm is standard gauge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a single-bay, single-storey portal frame with both bases fixed is:
questions.solution: A fixed-base single-bay portal frame is indeterminate to the third degree.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 550 mm, clear cover 30 mm and 25 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the IS 456 limit state stress block, the line of action of the compressive force lies from the extreme compression fibre at:
questions.solution: The centroid of the IS 456 stress block is at 0.42xu from the extreme fibre.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 100 MPa and 20 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 100 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{100+20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=60$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The final setting time of ordinary Portland cement should not exceed:
questions.solution: IS limits the final setting time of OPC to 600 minutes (10 hours).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average domestic per-capita water demand in Indian cities is taken as about:
questions.solution: A domestic demand of about 135 lpcd is commonly assumed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A pin-ended column (E = 210 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, length 4 m) has an Euler buckling load of about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A pin-ended column buckles at the Euler critical load with effective length equal to the actual length.
**Formula.** $P_{cr}=\tfrac{\pi^2 E I}{L^2}$
**Given.** E = 210 GPa, I = 16×10⁻⁶ m⁴, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{\pi^2\times210\times10^9\times16\times10^{-6}}{4^2}$ N.
**Answer.** $P_{cr}\approx 2072.6$ kN.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy grade line lies above the hydraulic grade line by an amount equal to the:
questions.solution: EGL = HGL + velocity head (V²/2g).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam with a central point load W over span L, the magnitude of the fixed-end moment is:
questions.solution: Fixed-end moment for a central point load is WL/8 at each support.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a well-graded soil, the coefficient of curvature Cc lies between:
questions.solution: Well-graded soils have a high uniformity coefficient and Cc between 1 and 3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A total station essentially combines a theodolite with:
questions.solution: A total station integrates an electronic theodolite with an EDM and data recorder.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Using Manning's formula with n = 0.02, hydraulic radius R = 1.2 m and slope S = 0.0016, the mean velocity is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Manning's equation gives mean velocity from roughness, hydraulic radius and bed slope.
**Formula.** $V=\tfrac1n R^{2/3} S^{1/2}$
**Given.** n = 0.02, R = 1.2 m, S = 0.0016.
**Working.** $=\tfrac1{0.02}\times1.2^{2/3}\times\sqrt{0.0016}$.
**Answer.** $V\approx 2.26$ m/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The centre of pressure on a vertical immersed plane surface lies:
questions.solution: Due to increasing pressure with depth, the centre of pressure is below the centroid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 10 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 10 kN/m, L = 6 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{10\times6^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=45$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The fundamental principle of surveying is to work:
options: From the part to the whole
From the whole to the part
questions.solution: Surveying proceeds from whole to part to control accumulation of errors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Manning's formula is used for:
questions.solution: Manning's equation estimates uniform flow velocity in open channels.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RL of a benchmark is 95.5 m. With back sight 1.2 m and fore sight 2.2 m, the RL of the next point is:
questions.solution: RL = 95.5 + 1.2 − 2.2 = 94.5 m (rise and fall).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Terzaghi's bearing capacity factor Nc for φ = 0 (strip footing) is:
questions.solution: For φ = 0, Terzaghi's Nc = 5.7.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 6 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 15 kN/m over its full span. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a simply supported beam peaks at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{8}$
**Given.** w = 15 kN/m, L = 6 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{15\times6^2}{8}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=67.5$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The instrument used to measure horizontal and vertical angles is the:
questions.solution: A theodolite measures both horizontal and vertical angles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The compound mainly responsible for early strength gain in cement is:
questions.solution: C3S hydrates rapidly and gives early strength to cement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 5 m clay layer (Cc = 0.4, e₀ = 1) under an initial effective stress of 100 kPa receives a 60 kPa increment. The primary consolidation settlement is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Primary consolidation of a normally consolidated clay follows the logarithmic compression law.
**Formula.** $S_c=\tfrac{C_c H}{1+e_0}\log_{10}\tfrac{\sigma_0+\Delta\sigma}{\sigma_0}$
**Given.** C_c = 0.4, H = 5 m, e₀ = 1, σ₀ = 100 kPa, Δσ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.4\times5000}{1+1}\log_{10}\tfrac{100+60}{100}$ mm.
**Answer.** $S_c\approx 204.1$ mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rigid pavements are generally made of:
questions.solution: Rigid pavements use a cement concrete slab that resists loads in flexure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 15 kPa and friction angle φ = 25°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 60 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 15 kPa, φ = 25°, σ = 60 kPa.
**Working.** $=15+60\times\tan 25^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=42.98$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The initial setting time of ordinary Portland cement should not be less than:
questions.solution: IS specifies a minimum initial setting time of 30 minutes for OPC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stopping sight distance depends on the driver's reaction time and the:
questions.solution: SSD = lag (reaction) distance + braking distance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The pressure at a depth of 10 m in water (ρ = 1000 kg/m³, g = 9.81 m/s²) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) indicates:
questions.solution: BOD measures the oxygen needed to biodegrade organic matter, indicating organic pollution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If LL = 50% and PL = 20%, the plasticity index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape of the ideal transition curve used on highways is a:
questions.solution: An ideal transition curve is a spiral where radius varies inversely with length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a floating body, stable equilibrium requires the metacentric height to be:
questions.solution: A positive metacentric height (M above G) ensures stable equilibrium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An axial load of 150 kN acts on a bar of cross-sectional area 500 mm². The direct stress is:
questions.solution: Stress = P/A = 150000 N / 500 mm² = 300 MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The CBR test is used in the design of:
questions.solution: California Bearing Ratio is used to design flexible pavement thickness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The thermal stress developed in a fully restrained bar for a temperature rise ΔT is:
questions.solution: Restrained thermal stress = αEΔT, independent of length and area.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Disinfection of municipal water supplies is most commonly achieved by:
questions.solution: Chlorination is the standard method for disinfecting drinking water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A hydraulic jump in an open channel forms when the flow changes from:
questions.solution: A hydraulic jump dissipates energy as the flow shifts from supercritical to subcritical.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a singly reinforced section (Fe415, M30 concrete, width 300 mm, effective depth 550 mm), the limiting moment of resistance (Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d²) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For a balanced (limiting) Fe415 section the moment of resistance is fixed by the coefficient 0.138 in $f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$.
**Formula.** $M_{u,lim}=0.138\,f_{ck}\,b\,d^2$
**Given.** f_ck = 30 MPa, b = 300 mm, d = 550 mm.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{0.138\times30\times300\times550^2}{10^6}$ kNm.
**Answer.** $M_{u,lim}\approx 375.7$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Superelevation is provided on:
questions.solution: Superelevation counters centrifugal force on horizontal curves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam with a central point load of 16 kN, the maximum shear force is:
questions.solution: Each reaction = W/2 = 8 kN, which is the maximum shear force.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The combined correction for curvature and refraction in levelling (d in km) is about:
questions.solution: Curvature (−0.0785 d²) and refraction (+0.0112 d²) combine to about −0.0673 d² m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coefficient of discharge Cd of an orifice equals:
questions.solution: Cd is the product of the coefficient of contraction and coefficient of velocity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A soil has cohesion c = 15 kPa and friction angle φ = 30°. By the Mohr–Coulomb criterion, its shear strength on a plane with normal stress 120 kPa is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mohr–Coulomb shear strength combines cohesion with frictional resistance proportional to normal stress.
**Formula.** $\tau=c+\sigma\tan\phi$
**Given.** c = 15 kPa, φ = 30°, σ = 120 kPa.
**Working.** $=15+120\times\tan 30^\circ$.
**Answer.** $\tau=84.28$ kPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The continuity equation for incompressible steady flow in a pipe is:
questions.solution: Conservation of mass gives A1V1 = A2V2 for incompressible flow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 20 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 20 kN, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{20\times4}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=20$ kNm at mid-span.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 165" width="320" height="165" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="40" y1="55" x2="280" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="3"/><polygon points="40,55 31,72 49,72" fill="#64748b"/><polygon points="280,55 271,72 289,72" fill="#64748b"/><line x1="160" y1="16" x2="160" y2="46" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="160,53 154,42 166,42" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="160" y="12" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">W = 20 kN</text><text x="160" y="88" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">span L = 4 m</text><polygon points="40,112 160,144 280,112" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.12)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="160" y="159" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">BMD — Mmax = 20 kNm</text></svg>
figure.caption: Simply supported beam — central point load

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Poisson's ratio for structural steel is approximately:
questions.solution: Steel has a Poisson's ratio of about 0.3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Curing of concrete is done primarily to:
questions.solution: Curing maintains moisture so cement hydration continues and concrete gains strength.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If E = 120 GPa and Poisson's ratio = 0.25, the modulus of rigidity G is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The activated sludge process is used for the treatment of:
questions.solution: The activated sludge process is an aerobic biological treatment for sewage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 5 m carries two equal 10 kN point loads placed 1 m from each support. The maximum bending moment (constant between the loads) is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two equal symmetric loads give equal reactions; between the loads the bending moment is constant at $R\cdot a$.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=P\,a$
**Given.** P = 10 kN, a = 1 m, span = 5 m.
**Working.** Each reaction $=10$ kN; $M_{max}=10\times1$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=10$ kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most common index organism for faecal contamination of water is:
questions.solution: E. coli is used as the indicator of faecal pollution in water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bernoulli's equation is based on the conservation of:
questions.solution: Bernoulli's theorem expresses conservation of energy along a streamline.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 40 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A central point load on a simply supported beam gives maximum bending moment at mid-span.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{W L}{4}$
**Given.** W = 40 kN, L = 4 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{40\times4}{4}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=40$ kNm at mid-span.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 165" width="320" height="165" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><line x1="40" y1="55" x2="280" y2="55" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="3"/><polygon points="40,55 31,72 49,72" fill="#64748b"/><polygon points="280,55 271,72 289,72" fill="#64748b"/><line x1="160" y1="16" x2="160" y2="46" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><polygon points="160,53 154,42 166,42" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="160" y="12" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">W = 40 kN</text><text x="160" y="88" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">span L = 4 m</text><polygon points="40,112 160,144 280,112" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.12)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="160" y="159" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">BMD — Mmax = 40 kNm</text></svg>
figure.caption: Simply supported beam — central point load

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coefficient of permeability of a soil has units of:
questions.solution: Permeability k has the dimensions of velocity, commonly cm/s.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 4 m carries a central point load of 16 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM = WL/4 = 16 × 4 / 4 = 16 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The actual size of a standard modular brick (without mortar) is:
questions.solution: The actual modular brick size is 190 × 90 × 90 mm; nominal size with mortar is 200 × 100 × 100 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Closely spaced contour lines indicate:
questions.solution: The closer the contours, the steeper the ground slope.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cross-sectional area of one 25 mm diameter bar is approximately:
questions.solution: Area = π/4 × 25² = 491 mm².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The limiting moment of resistance of a singly reinforced beam with Fe415 steel is:
questions.solution: For Fe415, Mu,lim = 0.138 fck b d².

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If E = 120 GPa and Poisson's ratio = 0.3, the modulus of rigidity G is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape factor of a rectangular cross-section in plastic bending is:
questions.solution: Shape factor = plastic modulus / elastic section modulus = 1.5 for a rectangle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The water-cement ratio law was proposed by:
questions.solution: Abrams' law relates concrete strength inversely to the water-cement ratio.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 110 MPa and 50 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 110 MPa, σ₃ = 50 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{110-50}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=30$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The classical bearing capacity theory for shallow footings was given by:
questions.solution: Terzaghi's bearing capacity theory is the standard for shallow foundations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever of span 3 m carries a uniformly distributed load of 5 kN/m over its entire length. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A full-span UDL on a cantilever produces its maximum moment at the fixed support.
**Formula.** $M_{max}=\tfrac{w L^2}{2}$
**Given.** w = 5 kN/m, L = 3 m.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{5\times3^2}{2}$.
**Answer.** $M_{max}=22.5$ kNm at the fixed end.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A venturimeter is used to measure:
questions.solution: A venturimeter measures discharge using the pressure difference between throat and inlet.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape factor of a solid circular section in plastic bending is about:
questions.solution: For a solid circular section the shape factor is about 1.7 (16/3π).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point the major and minor principal stresses are 100 MPa and 20 MPa. From Mohr's circle, the maximum in-plane shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The maximum in-plane shear stress equals the radius of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\tau_{max}=\tfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 100 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{100-20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\tau_{max}=40$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tacheometry is mainly used to determine:
options: Horizontal distances and elevations without chaining
questions.solution: Tacheometry measures distances and elevations rapidly using stadia readings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a point σx = 80 MPa, σy = 40 MPa and τxy = 20 MPa. The major principal stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The major principal stress is the centre of Mohr's circle plus its radius.
**Formula.** $\sigma_1=\tfrac{\sigma_x+\sigma_y}{2}+\sqrt{\left(\tfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$
**Given.** σx = 80 MPa, σy = 40 MPa, τxy = 20 MPa.
**Working.** Centre $=60$, radius $=28.28$; $\sigma_1=60+28.28$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_1=88.28$ MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The permissible pH range of drinking water as per IS 10500 is:
questions.solution: IS 10500 specifies a desirable pH of 6.5 to 8.5 for drinking water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The process of determining the relative elevations of points is called:
questions.solution: Levelling determines the relative heights (elevations) of points.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a void ratio e = 0.5, the porosity n is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A prismatic compass measures bearings in the:
questions.solution: A prismatic compass reads whole circle bearings from 0° to 360°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Rankine active earth pressure coefficient Ka for a soil with φ = 30° is:
questions.solution: Ka = (1−sinφ)/(1+sinφ) = 0.33 for φ = 30°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a beam section subjected to bending, the bending stress is zero at the:
questions.solution: Bending stress varies linearly and is zero at the neutral axis (centroidal axis).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness in water is caused mainly by salts of:
questions.solution: Dissolved calcium and magnesium salts are the main cause of hardness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 2.5 m² with velocity 4 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Loss of prestress due to elastic shortening is most significant in:
questions.solution: Elastic shortening loss is significant in pre-tensioning and sequential post-tensioning.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The velocity head corresponding to a velocity of 3 m/s is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flow in a circular pipe is laminar when the Reynolds number is:
questions.solution: Pipe flow is laminar for Re below roughly 2000.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Block shear failure of a bolted tension member involves:
options: Tension rupture on one plane and shear rupture on another
questions.solution: Block shear tears out a block by tension on one plane and shear on the perpendicular plane.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The principal stresses at a point are 80 MPa and 20 MPa. The normal stress on the plane carrying the maximum shear stress is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** On the plane of maximum shear the normal stress equals the centre of Mohr's circle.
**Formula.** $\sigma_n=\tfrac{\sigma_1+\sigma_3}{2}$
**Given.** σ₁ = 80 MPa, σ₃ = 20 MPa.
**Working.** $=\tfrac{80+20}{2}$.
**Answer.** $\sigma_n=50$ MPa.

figure.caption: Centre of Mohr's circle = mean normal stress

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Traffic volume is expressed as:
options: Number of vehicles per unit time
questions.solution: Traffic volume is the number of vehicles crossing a section per unit time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular channel 4 m wide carries 10 m³/s. The critical depth (yc = (q²/g)^{1/3}) is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Critical depth in a rectangular channel depends only on the discharge per unit width q.
**Formula.** $y_c=\left(\tfrac{q^2}{g}\right)^{1/3}$
**Given.** Q = 10 m³/s, B = 4 m, g = 9.81 m/s².
**Working.** $q=\tfrac{10}{4}=2.5$ m²/s; $y_c=\left(\tfrac{2.5^2}{9.81}\right)^{1/3}$.
**Answer.** $y_c\approx 0.86$ m.
